modules.title: Grade Rate Table Configuration
modules.notes:
Reviewer must check:
- The order of entries matches the original FILLER lines (G1..G7 then G8); any deviation breaks the MOVE-based initialization that expects identical layout.
- The OCCURS clause on WS-GRADE-TABLE must be changed from 7 to 8 in the modernized source so that the table can hold all 8 entries.
- Downstream code that iterates with a hardcoded number (e.g., a report) must be updated to use the array length or the new constant.
- All rates remain in pence (integer) to avoid floating-point drift; decimal conversion only when producing a pound value for output.

modules.title: Grade Hourly Rate Lookup
modules.notes:
Reviewer must verify:
- The function replaces the PERFORM VARYING loop; the loop bound in COBOL (WS-IX > 7) must be updated to 8 or removed entirely when using this function.
- The original code leaves WS-BASE-RATE unchanged if grade not found (no error). This function returns undefined to signal same ‘not found’ condition.
- The pension classification in paragraph 550-PENSION correctly treats G8 as standard (5% EE, 3% ER) without code change; no change needed there.
- Any other occurrences of a hardcoded grade count (e.g., in reports) must be updated to use the length of GRADE_RATES.
- Rounding and arithmetic are not affected; this lookup only provides an integer rate in pence.
